records.ety: [OE. feinen, F. feindre (p. pr. feignant), fr. L. fingere; akin to L. figura figure,and E. dough. See Dough, and cf. Figure, Faint, Effigy, Fiction.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Feigned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Feigning.]

defs: To give a mental existence to, as to something not real or actual; to imagine; to invent; hence, to pretend; to form and relate as if true.
quotes: There are no such things done as thou sayest, but thou feignest them out of thine own heart.
The poet Did feign that Orpheus drew trees, stones, and floods.

defs: To represent by a false appearance of; to pretend; to counterfeit; as, to feign a sickness.
